description_long: This radiator features a long, rectangular body with a series of evenly spaced, vertical cylindrical columns running along its main face, designed to maximize surface area for heat dissipation. The top surface is flat and perforated with a grid of small holes, likely for ventilation and heat release. The ends of the radiator are squared off, with one end slightly extended and angled, possibly to accommodate control mechanisms or pipe connections. The base is mounted on a flat, brown platform, suggesting a floor-standing installation. The overall finish is smooth and uniform, with a matte white color dominating the exterior. No visible valves or adjustment knobs are present, and the design is utilitarian, focusing on function over decorative elements.
description: A long, white radiator with vertical cylindrical columns and a perforated top, mounted on a brown base. Its smooth, matte finish and utilitarian design emphasize efficient heat dissipation.
description_view_0: The main face of the radiator is visible, showing the full row of vertical cylindrical columns and the perforated top surface.
description_view_1: A narrow end view, highlighting the depth of the radiator and the rounded ends of the columns.
description_view_2: The opposite main face, nearly identical to view 0, with the same vertical columns and perforated top.
description_view_3: The other narrow end, showing the squared-off end and the slight extension on one side.
attribute_pipe_connections: none visible; likely at the ends or rear, but not shown
